old_claim.note: Advanced Chemistry provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Phosphoramidite oligonucleotide synthesis is a chemical synthesis method using protected nucleoside reagents and controlled coupling chemistry.

source_shape.source_locator: PubMed record for Caruthers et al., Deoxyoligonucleotide synthesis via the phosphoramidite method.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source identifies deoxyoligonucleotide synthesis as a phosphoramidite chemical method.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The scoped node represents practical synthetic oligonucleotide production; for that scope, controlled nucleic-acid chemistry is not just context but the production method itself.

ontology_before: DNA synthesis and oligonucleotides treated advanced chemistry as a generic enabling context with a CRISPR Nobel source that did not support the synthesis method.
ontology_after: DNA synthesis and oligonucleotides are scoped to phosphoramidite oligonucleotide synthesis, making advanced chemistry the required production method while DNA structure and molecular biology remain enabling context.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: dna_synthesis_oligonucleotides -> advanced_chemistry is required rather than generic enabling, while DNA structure and molecular biology remain enabling context.
would_reject_if: The node were broadened to natural DNA replication or enzymatic DNA copying without retaining synthetic oligonucleotide chemistry as the scope.
The edge note implied that all downstream genome-editing uses require this exact phosphoramidite method rather than synthetic oligonucleotides as a practical platform.
A future split separated chemical oligonucleotide synthesis from broader DNA synthesis and moved this required edge only to the chemical-synthesis node.
short_reason: Practical synthetic oligonucleotide production is a chemical synthesis technology, so advanced chemistry is a required method dependency for this scoped node.
